title_en: World of Warcraft and RuneScape Fans Want Official Jimothy Support After Game Devs Show Love for Viral Raccoon
summary_en: The internet has fallen in love with a viral raccoon named Jimothy, and so has the gaming industry.
body_en:
The internet has fallen in love with a viral raccoon named Jimothy, and so has the gaming industry.
In case you've somehow dodged this adorable little friend on social media, Jimothy is a small raccoon in Seattle who has scurried his way through X/Twitter, Reddit, Facebook, and just about every other online platform you can think of. His unique size, seemingly caused by what many believe is a rare condition called Short Spine Syndrome, has made him hard to miss, and almost impossible to forget.
Locals seem to have taken notice of this specific masked critter before, but it wasn't until Instagram user Kiana Hall captured a video that he became the latest internet-famous animal. She told The Seattle Times that she came up with his name because he simply "looked like a Jimothy." The millions of social media impressions, hundreds of fan edits, and countless stories he's racked up since came naturally.
Jimothy with his family as a baby pic.twitter.com/H9MHr00txI
Jimothy has Officially Made it to the Big Leagues ❤️⚾️ #DUBSEA pic.twitter.com/qO8wBEbza2
Forget spices, I'd go to war for Jimothy pic.twitter.com/glkHwiTewI
The internet embraced Jimothy the raccoon so quickly that he's become inescapable in less than a week since Hall's original post. Most recently, some game developers have started to show the love with more than just a simple post or two.
For example, you probably shouldn't be surprised to hear Dee Dee Creations, the studio behind 2024's Squirrel With a Gun , put together a Jimothy skin tribute for their game. This is a miraculous 43-second video that arms the viral raccoon, making him more powerful than ever before.
Perhaps the most popular take on Jimothy in the gaming world comes from Blizzard Entertainment, which created a render for what the stout creature would look like in World of Warcraft . Old School RuneScape then followed suit with a rough (but still very cute) upload.
🔫🦝Jimothy tribute 🔫🦝 pic.twitter.com/CqJbz23R1k
Jimothy ❤️ https://t.co/MKhyfCm2mq pic.twitter.com/2LtV3E2eqX
Jimothy 🦝❤️ https://t.co/2s8Rm4pHs3 pic.twitter.com/AI8PZbwdMu
Despite being relatively high-effort posts, it doesn't look like Jimothy is actually available to download in any of the aforementioned games. Dee Dee, for example, admitted in a follow-up post that this was a quick mod for a pre-existing raccoon skin and not actually something available to all players.
Do players still want Jimothy in their games? Of course.
"Make him a battle pet, and a big Jim for a mount," one World of Warcraft player suggested .
"The people want a Jimothy Rocky transmog," a RuneScape player proclaimed .
"If they add him I hope they make him unattackable," another with a sad face.
pic.twitter.com/ASG5rLUm1y
For now, Jimothy will just have to live on as another addition in the history book of viral memes. For more, you can read about how Tekken 8 tapped into a viral video of a man punching a kangaroo with its own crossover .
